raw.objective: “to develop a quantitative approach that characterizes the magnitude of and variability in phonemic generative fluency scores as measured by the Controlled Oral Word Association (COWA) test in healthy volunteers after administration of an oral and a novel intravenous (IV) formulation of topiramate (TPM)”
raw.covariates: PK Tested: Age, Sex, WT, Race Included: all PK parameters were scaled allometrically by WT (fixed exponents) PKPD NCOWA≥4 (Number of times the COWA test was administered to the volunteers) included on baseline parameter
raw.observations: This analysis quantified the effect of TPM exposure on generative verbal fluency as measured by COWA. The model predicts a 27% reduction in the COWA score at the average observed maximal plasma concentration after a 100 mg dose of TPM. The single-dose administration of relatively low TPM doses and narrow range of resultant concentrations limits the extrapolation of the PK-PD relationship to higher TPM exposures
raw.dose: Multiple oral and IV doses in healthy subjects
raw.structure: PK: 2-compartment linear model with first-order absorption and elimination PKPD: exponential decline function
raw.equations: PK: CL = theta1*((WT/70)**0.75)*exp(eta1) Vc = theta2*((WT/70)**1)*exp(eta3) Q = theta3*(WT/70)**0.75 Vp = theta4*(WT/70)**1 Ka = theta5*exp(eta3) F1 = theta6 Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps1) If (ROUTE==0) Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps2) PKPD: COWAij = BLi * exp(-KE*Cij) BL = theta1*(theta2** NCOWA≥4)*exp(eta1) KE = theta3 Cobs = Cipred + eps1 ROUTE is route of administration (0=IV, 1=oral)
raw.parameter_values: [PK] theta1 = 1.21 theta2 = 59.3 theta3 = 1.02 theta4 = 12.1 theta5 = 2.38 theta6 = 1.08 omega1 = 0.037 omega2 = 0.058 omega3 = 0.25 sigma1 = 0.033 sigma2 = 0.005 [PKPD] theta1 = 42.5 theta2 = 1.12 theta3 = 0.157 omega1 = 0.028 sigma1 = 7.1

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.implementation: Canonical structure is normalized first; ADVAN/TRANS is then deterministically inferred with provenance and alternatives.
normalization.population: Free-text cohorts and summary statistics are retained and additionally normalized into structured cohort descriptors.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
